description: FilmCrop detection engine core pipeline: (1) detect coarse frame boundaries via local-peak matching, (2) apply 3:2/2:3 aspect-ratio constraint using highest-confidence edges as anchor, (3) refine boundaries under ratio constraint to align with true film edges. Plus reliability fixes for Lightroom deployment.

userStories.title: Detect coarse frame boundaries with local-peak matching and consistency enforcement
userStories.description: As the detection engine, I need to find approximate gap centers between film frames reliably across different film types, so that the subsequent aspect-ratio constraint has a solid anchor.
acceptanceCriteria: find_boundaries() uses _find_local_peaks() to enumerate all local extrema
Peaks are matched to theoretical positions (±25% pitch window) with distance-weighted scoring
_enforce_boundary_consistency() nudges boundaries toward median pitch while preserving monotonicity
All three test images produce gap_edges with width diff ≤ 10px
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified: gap diff=0px on all 3 images

userStories.title: Apply 3:2/2:3 aspect-ratio constraint using highest-confidence edges
userStories.description: As the detection engine, I want to use the most reliable dimension (median frame height from gap analysis) to derive the theoretical frame width via 3:2 ratio, so that crops match standard 35mm film proportions regardless of scanning artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: _detect_long_edges_proportional() computes target_span = median_frame_dim / aspect_ratio
Target window is searched on cross-axis projection for maximum variance (content detection)
Proportional result is preferred when span ≥ direct_detection_span × 0.92 and ≥ min_reasonable
All three test images have long_edges within 5% of proportional target
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified: long span deviation 0.0%-3.4% on all 3 images

userStories.title: Refine boundaries under ratio constraint using gradient search
userStories.description: As the detection engine, I want to perform a local gradient search near the proportional boundaries without breaking the aspect ratio, so that the final crop edges align exactly with the true film content boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: _refine_long_edges() searches ±search_margin for steepest gradient
Refinement is rejected if new_deviation_from_target > old_deviation (ratio protection)
Refinement is rejected if shift > max(20px, 5% target_span)
Refinement is rejected if edges are pushed into image margins
All three test images have refined edges matching proportional within 2px
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified: refine diff=0px on all 3 images

userStories.title: Fix scan-edge detection for film strips scanned flush to image borders
userStories.description: As a user with film strips that have no visible black border at top/bottom, I want scan edges to be detected correctly so that partial frames are not cropped.
acceptanceCriteria: _detect_scan_edge handles flat-gradient cases where border_mean is not below mid_mean threshold
luckyc20013.tif first_offset > 0 (currently returns 0)
52191.tif and 52194.tif scan edges remain unchanged (regression test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Prevent Python bytecode caching from masking source changes in Lightroom
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Python code changes to take effect immediately in Lightroom without manual cache clearing, so that algorithm updates are reliably deployed.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_thumb.py sets sys.dont_write_bytecode = True before any imports
ProcessAgent.lua injects PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 into shell commands
All existing __pycache__ directories under FilmCrop_Clean.lrplugin are removed
Running detection does not recreate __pycache__
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified: no __pycache__ recreated after detection runs

userStories.title: Add batch validation test for core detection pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an automated test that verifies the full detection pipeline on all test images, so that regressions are caught before release.
acceptanceCriteria: test_detect_batch.py runs analyze_image on all .tif files in test_files/
Reports: gap width diff, frame height CV, long edge span vs proportional target
Fails if gap width diff > 10px OR frame height CV > 5% OR long edges not proportional
Exit code 0 on pass, non-zero on failure
Typecheck passes
